examples.title: Dijkstra's Algorithm Implementation
examples.description: Python implementation using adjacency list and heapq for priority queue
examples.explanation: Uses heapq (binary heap) for efficient minimum extraction. The algorithm maintains a visited set to handle duplicates in priority queue. Distance updates trigger new entries in the heap.

examples.title: Dijkstra's Algorithm in C
examples.description: C implementation using adjacency matrix and arrays
examples.explanation: Uses adjacency matrix and simple array scanning to find minimum distance vertex. This approach has O(V²) time complexity but is simple to implement and works well for dense graphs.

examples.title: Dijkstra's Algorithm in Java
examples.description: Java implementation using PriorityQueue and adjacency list
examples.explanation: Java implementation using PriorityQueue with custom Comparator. The Node class implements Comparable for priority queue ordering. Uses adjacency list representation suitable for sparse graphs.

examples.title: Dijkstra's Algorithm Pseudocode
examples.description: Standard pseudocode showing the core logic of Dijkstra's Algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Dijkstra(Graph G, Vertex source)
BEGIN
    // Initialize distances and visited set
    FOR each vertex v IN G.V DO
        dist[v] ← INFINITY
        visited[v] ← FALSE
        parent[v] ← NULL
    END FOR
    
    dist[source] ← 0
    
    // Priority queue containing (distance, vertex) pairs
    PQ ← empty priority queue
    INSERT(PQ, (0, source))
    
    WHILE PQ IS NOT EMPTY DO
        // Extract vertex with minimum distance
        (d, u) ← EXTRACT-MIN(PQ)
        
        IF visited[u] = TRUE THEN
            CONTINUE  // Skip if already processed
        END IF
        
        visited[u] ← TRUE
        
        // Relax all edges from u
        FOR each edge (u, v, weight) IN G.adj[u] DO
            IF visited[v] = FALSE AND dist[u] + weight < dist[v] THEN
                dist[v] ← dist[u] + weight
                parent[v] ← u
                INSERT(PQ, (dist[v], v))
            END IF
        END FOR
    END WHILE
    
    RETURN dist, parent
END

// To reconstruct path from source to destination
ALGORITHM ReconstructPath(parent, destination)
BEGIN
    path ← EMPTY LIST
    current ← destination
    
    WHILE current ≠ NULL DO
        INSERT path at beginning
        current ← parent[current]
    END WHILE
    
    RETURN path
END
examples.explanation: The pseudocode shows initialization, the main loop extracting minimum distance vertex, relaxation of edges, and path reconstruction using parent pointers. The algorithm terminates when all reachable vertices are processed.
examples.timeComplexity: O((V + E) log V) with binary heap
